headline: Mongolian Desert Doc 'Colors of White Rock' Lands Global Sales Deal
lede: MetFilm Sales swoops in on Khoroldorj Choijoovanchig's debut documentary, securing worldwide distribution rights ahead of its Tribeca Film Festival premiere.
body:
In a strategic move that highlights the growing global appetite for environmental storytelling, London-based MetFilm Sales has acquired the worldwide sales rights to Mongolian filmmaker Khoroldorj Choijoovanchig's powerful documentary "Colors of White Rock."

The film offers a sweeping visual narrative of Mongolia's Gobi Desert, exposing the landscape's transformation under the country's intense mining boom — a phenomenon locals have dubbed "Minegolia." Choijoovanchig's debut feature promises an unflinching look at the environmental and cultural disruptions caused by industrial extraction.

Set to world premiere in the official documentary competition at the upcoming Tribeca Film Festival, the documentary represents a critical exploration of industrial impact on traditional landscapes. MetFilm Sales sees significant potential in the project, positioning it as a compelling international documentary with urgent environmental themes.

The acquisition signals growing international interest in documentary works that bridge cultural perspectives with global ecological concerns. Choijoovanchig's lens captures a nuanced portrayal of technological progress and environmental consequence, offering audiences a rare glimpse into Mongolia's complex relationship with industrial development.

As documentary markets continue to expand, "Colors of White Rock" stands poised to make significant waves in international film circles.
key_facts: Debut documentary from Mongolian filmmaker Khoroldorj Choijoovanchig
Worldwide sales rights acquired by MetFilm Sales
World premiere at Tribeca Film Festival documentary competition
Explores Mongolia's mining impact on Gobi Desert landscape
